concepts.name: Inflation
concepts.definition: The general increase in prices of goods and services over time, which reduces the purchasing power of money.

concepts.name: Purchasing Power
concepts.definition: The value of money expressed in terms of the amount of goods and services that one unit of money can buy.

concepts.name: Consumer Price Index (CPI)
concepts.definition: A measure of the average change over time in the prices paid by urban consumers for a fixed basket of goods and services reflecting typical household consumption patterns.

concepts.name: GDP Deflator
concepts.definition: A measure of the change in prices of all domestically produced goods and services in an economy, calculated as the ratio of nominal GDP to real GDP, using a changing basket based on current production.

concepts.name: Fixed-Income Household
concepts.definition: A household that receives a constant income, such as pensions or fixed salaries, which does not adjust with inflation.

concepts.name: Variable-Income Household
concepts.definition: A household with income that can adjust with inflation, such as wages increasing with cost of living or business revenues rising with prices.

concepts.name: Household Budget Strategy
concepts.definition: A plan or approach adopted by households to manage the impact of inflation on their budgets, including adjustments to spending, income, savings, and investments.

concepts.name: Macro Policy Tools
concepts.definition: Monetary and fiscal policies used by central banks and governments to manage inflation and economic activity.

concepts.name: Depth of Strategy
concepts.definition: The thoroughness with which a household plans for inflation effects, including detailed adjustments and scenario analysis.

concepts.name: Scenario Analysis
concepts.definition: The use of realistic examples or hypothetical situations to evaluate the effectiveness of household strategies under inflation.

concepts.name: Judgement Criteria
concepts.definition: Rubrics or standards applied to assess the quality and effectiveness of household strategies, including whether they are guided, independently reasoned, verified, and transferable.

concepts.name: Transferability
concepts.definition: The ability of a household to apply their inflation management strategy to different inflation scenarios or economic conditions.

relationships.note: Because income does not adjust with inflation
Because income can adjust with inflation

processes.name: Evaluating Household Strategies Under Inflation
steps: Assess the depth of the household's strategy for managing inflation effects.
Apply scenario analysis using realistic examples such as the Maya scenario.
Use judgement criteria to evaluate whether the strategy is worked through with guidance, independently reasoned, verified, and transferable.
Determine the effectiveness and adaptability of the strategy for different inflation scenarios.

misconceptions.name: CPI and GDP Deflator Measure the Same Inflation
misconceptions.description: Confusing CPI and GDP deflator as identical measures of inflation, whereas CPI uses a fixed basket of consumer goods and GDP deflator uses a changing basket of all domestically produced goods and services.

misconceptions.name: Inflation Affects All Households Equally
misconceptions.description: Assuming inflation impacts fixed-income and variable-income households in the same way, ignoring that fixed-income households are more vulnerable due to lack of income adjustment.

misconceptions.name: Macro Policy Tools Directly Manage Household Budgets
misconceptions.description: Believing that macroeconomic policies directly control household budgeting strategies, whereas their effects on households are indirect.
